ead.archdesc:
======= RMM08069Black Panther collection8069Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
aframafrcaThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:06:53 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Black Panther collection
Black Panther Party
Kim, Il-sŏng, 1912-1994.
Newton, Huey P.
Davis, Angela Y. (Angela Yvonne), 1944- (Title of work: If they come in the morning..)
8069
1.5 cubic feet. 1.5 cubic feet.
circa 1967-1973. Extent is approximate. Printed materials and publications. Publications, posters, handbills, flyers and news clippings covering the activities of the Black Panthers and several of its members. Publications include issues of the periodicals The Black Panther, Black Panther community newsletter, Liberated guardian, Open city, and The Berkeley tribe, and pamphlets by Huey P. Newton and Kim Il Sung. Many of the posters, handbills, and flyers concern Huey P. Newton, Bobby Seale, or Eldridge Cleaver. Other items include a cover proof for If they come in the morning by Angela Y. Davis. Collection material in English 7694772
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Fredrika Loew
Date completed:
June 2017
EAD encoding:
Fredrika Loew, June 2017
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, February 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Publications, posters, handbills, flyers and news clippings covering the activities of the Black Panthers and several of its members. Publications include issues of the periodicals The Black Panther, Black Panther community newsletter, Liberated guardian, Open city, and The Berkeley tribe, and pamphlets by Huey P. Newton and Kim Il Sung. Many of the posters, handbills, and flyers concern Huey P. Newton, Bobby Seale, or Eldridge Cleaver. Other items include a cover proof for If they come in the morning by Angela Y. Davis.
Cite As:
Black Panther Collection, #8069. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Fliers (printed matter). Posters. Newton, Huey P. Seale, Bobby, 1936- Cleaver, Eldridge, 1935-1998. Black Panther Party
7694772 <<<<<<< HEAD Series I. PublicationsSubseries Ia. Black Panther Community News ServiceNo. 4, You Can't Jail a Revolution1969-09-0162Vol. 1, No. 6, Huey Must be Set Free!1967-11-2362Vol. 3, No. 21, Next Victim of Fascist Frameup?1969-09-1362Vol. 4, No. 3, By lifting their hands against these revolutionaries1969-12-2041
Scope and Contents
Year misidentified on cover as 1967Vol. 2, No. 2, Free All Black Political Prisoners1968-05-0442Vol. 2, No. 23, Happy Birthday Huey1969-02-1743Vol. 2, No. 25, Viva Zapata 1883-19191969-03-0944Vol. 2, No. 28, Chairman Bobby Seale Returns From Scandinavia1969-03-3145Vol. 3, No. 8, Capitalism Plus Racism Breeds Fascism1969-06-1446Vol. 3, No. 9, Reagan's Fascist Pigs Vamp on Sacramento HQ1969-06-2147Vol. 3, No. 11, Eldridge Cleaver Breaks His Silence1969-07-0548Vol. 3, No. 121969-07-1249Vol. 3, No. 14, United Against Fascism1969-07-26410Vol. 3, No. 22, Our Fight Is Not in Vietnam1969-09-20411Vol 3, No. 24, Bobby Seale, Chairman1969-10-0462Vol. 3, No. 28, Political Prisoners of USA Fascism1969-11-01412Vol. 4, No. 4, Although my body may be bound and shackled…1969-12-27413Vol. 4, No. 8, The Invincible Thoughts of The Black Panther Party Can Never Be Destroyed By Fire Nor Water,1970-01-31414Vol. 4, No. 10, Happy Birthday Huey1970-02-17415Vol. 4, No. 14, Hunter's Point 1967; Hunter's Point 19701970-03-07416Vol. 4, No. 18, He Was The Beginning: Lil Bobby Hutton1970-04-06417Vol. 4, No. 20, "This Is But An Aspect Of The Unfolding Picture Of American Fascism,"1970-04-1851Vol. 4, No. 22, 'The Black Panther Party is the People's Party and We Are Primarily Interested in Freeing Man.'1970-05-0252Vol. 4, No. 24, The Heirs of Malcolm Have Picked Up The Gun and Now Stand Millions Strong Facing The Racist Pig Oppressor1970-05-1953Vol. 4, No. 25 and 26, Babylon,1970-05-3154Vol. 5, No. 7, Revolutionary Suicide1970-08-1555Vol. 5, No. 8, If The Penalty for the Quest for Freedom is Death—Then by Death We Escape to Freedom1970-08-2156Vol. 5, No. 10, Lonnie McLucas1970-09-0557Vol. 5, No. 17, Rent-A-Pid Kills 14 Year Old Brother In Homemade Playground1970-10-2458Vol. 5, No. 20, On The Battlefield In Cairo, Illinois1970-11-1459Vol. 5, No. 24, New York City…Criminal Slumlords Are Crime Partners With City Government Officials and Courts1970-12-26510Vol. 5, No. 26, The Last Christmas???1970-12-14511Vol. 5, No. 28, In the spirit of revolutionary solidarity1971-01-09512Subseries Ia. Black Panther Intercommunal News ServiceVol. 6, No. 5, The San Quentin Branch of The Black Panther Party Opens!1971-02-27513Vol. 6, No. 9, The World is Yours, As Well As Ours1971-03-27514Vol. 6, No. 11, Black Genocide: Sickle Cell Anemia1971-04-10515Vol. 7. No. 12, World War III?1971-11-13516Vol. 7, No. 19, Cease-fire Verdict in L.A.1972-01-01517Vol. 7, No. 25, Another Mother For Struggle1972-02-12520Vol. 7, No. 28. Welcome Home, Angela Davis1972-03-04521Vol. 7, No. 29, The Glass Cage Must Come Down!1971-08-1462Vol. 8, No. 5, No Eviction, Free Repairs—If You Were Your Own Landlord1972-04-22522Vol. 8, No. 21, Survival Pending Liberation1972-08-12523Vol. 9, No. 26, Vote April 17th1973-04-1462Center spread only, "24 Hour Vigils for Eldridge Cleaver"1968-12-071Subseries Ia. advertising march in Queens, New York to free the Panther 21, Bobby Seale, and all political prisonersHumanity, Freedom, PeaceNew World Liberation Front|Afro-American Liberation Army1971317Black News, Vol. 2, No. 11973-04-01318Angela Davis Newsletter: Harris Still Fails to Implicate AngelaPhiladelphia Committee To Free Angela Davis1972Political newsletter21
Scope and Contents
Contains article regarding Angela Davis' charges and trial in regards to the Soledad Brothers, as well as a list of happenings (rallies, fundraisers, and other events), and bookstores related to the Black Panther Party in Philadelphia.John Huggins Free Hot Breakfast Program for Children1970Poster|Mimeograph22Sickle Cell Anemia1970-1974Flyer23The Black Panther Party, William Kunstler, and Dave Dellinger are Not Speaking at Bryant Park Today Because…1970Flyer24The Black Panther Intercommunal New ServiceThis special issue of the Black Panther Party newspaper was printed in Vietnamese and features an illustration of a member of the National Liberation Front (Viet Cong)1971-12-2625
Scope and Contents
No. 5 (Vietnamese)Free David Hilliard, Free All Political Prisoners1971Poster|Screenprint26
Scope and Contents
Text on poster reads, "Railroaded to prison: June 12, 1971. There were no Black People on the jury in the case of David Hilliard, although 38% of the Oakland Community is Black.""The Black Panther Party 40th Year Reunion and Celebration"2006Poster27
Scope and Contents
Contains full color reproduction of illustration by Emory Douglas, Ministry of Culture for the Black Panther Party, and information regarding the Black Panther Party reunion in Oakland, California. Two copies.Here and Now For Bobby Seale1970Poster|Screenprint28
Scope and Contents
Text reads, "Here and Now For Bobby Seale, We must save Bobby Seale because we must save the Black Panther Party because we must save the revolutionary spirit in America."On the 13th Anniversary of the Assassination of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., Join Us In A March Against Racist Violence!1981Poster|Screenprint29Ramparts1970Poster210"War Sucks" buttonMU-1744Undated319"All Power to The People" and photograph of Huey P. Newton buttonMU-1745Undated319Button with sepia print photograph of Bobby Hutton, first member of the Black Panther PartyMU-1746Undated319Button with black peace sign on white background.MU-1747Undated319Button with sepia print photograph of Eldridge CleaverMU-1748Undated319 ======= Series I.
